mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and reducing forward equity returns as investors price in higher recession probabilities.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal exhibits no predictive power beyond a random walk benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing construction activity combined with robust employment growth indicates sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with forward returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows, indicating decoupling from equity fundamentals.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects increased manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which correlates with higher corporate earnings per share and generates positive short-term equity market returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year period, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when industrial output accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals peak corporate profitability and strong consumer spending, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns average negative values when GDP growth exceeds the threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and elevated federal funds rates compress real yields and equity risk premiums, forcing portfolio rebalancing away from equities and generating negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive in over 50% of high-inflation/high-rate episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail sales volumes reflects resilient consumer spending behavior, providing a stable earnings floor for consumer-facing corporations and supporting positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no directional relationship with retail sales momentum over a 10-year backtest, or the signal's hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll growth during a late-cycle expansion indicates wage-driven consumption that sustains corporate profit margins, driving positive forward equity returns as earnings revisions remain upwardly biased.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when payroll growth exceeds 200k monthly for three consecutive months, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over any 5-year rolling window.
